original_text:
I had a conference in Tampa and made a vacation out of it and spent some extra time in town. My good buddy lives in Tampa and we decided to hit up Busch Gardens on a weekday. The nice thing about visiting an amusement park on a weekday is that the lines are so much shorter than what you would experience on a weekend. While we were there, that meant less than 5 minutes of waiting time per ride, and oftentimes, even less than that or no wait at all. So went all out!

We rode every single rollercoaster and really any ride that seemed halfway entertaining. We hit them all in rapid succession to the point where my buddy threw up on one of the rollercoasters. I was dying laughing! I hate waiting in line so being able to breeze through things was a big plus. In between the rides, it was nice to check out the animals too. The highlight for me were the lions. Busch Gardens is African safari-themed and you can pay extra to go on a safari and get closer to the animals. We didn't do that but still enjoyed checking them out from a distance. We had some food too. Your typical amusement park fare. Nothing special.

I have to knock off a star for a couple of reasons. One, tickets were more expensive than I expected. In addition to your ticket, you have to pay for parking too so it ends up being a little pricey. A few of the rides were also closed while we were there. And not just one or two but several popular rides. They didn't mention all of the rides that were closed when we were paying to get in which I think is something that should be displayed prominently so people know what they're getting into. Or, at the very least, offer a discount if a significant number of rides are closed down.

Don't get me wrong, I had a good time. We even experienced a torrential downpour and didn't have umbrellas to help us out. All part of the experience.

original_text:
We haven't been to Busch Gardens since a Hawl-O-Scream visit several years ago and were looking forward to attending the park during their annual Christmas Town Event (Nov 12 thru Jan 2). As I mentioned in my recent review of SeaWorld Orlando, on Black Friday 2021, we purchased our SeaWorld & Busch Gardens Tampa two park pass (for two) totaling $131.98, definitely a great deal!

SeaWorld is the parent company to Busch Gardens, so my 3-complaints from my SeaWorld review remain. (1) $30 parking is expensive, why not include this into the cost of the ticket? (2) Food and drink prices can be excessive, $10.99 for a beer, $4.70 for bottled water, $6 for a cookie WTF? (3) once again, no paper map. Guests must use a QR code to download a map or take a photo at the entrance, then continuously use your phone to navigate the park, this is beyond frustrating. Do yourself a favor and download the map from their website and print a copy or two to take with you.

Busch Gardens has a good variety of rides to choose from, and they are all a blast. Unfortunately, on this occasion, Skyride, Congo River Rapids, Cheetah Hunt, and Kumba were closed for repair. In addition, Falcon's Fury closed early, Montu closed down due to heavy rainfall (of course, as were we next in line), and the Iron Gwazi does not open until March 2022. As you can see, we didn't have the best run with roller coasters on this visit, but we still had fun on Tigris, Cobra's Curse, Sheikra, and the Stanley Falls Flume.

I enjoyed that Busch Gardens has a balanced selection of rides, animal encounters, and activities to choose from. There is a lot to do here for anyone, and you can easily spend an entire day exploring. We came for the whole Christmas experience, and it was beautiful, but some of the Festive Shows, such as the 'Christmas on Ice,' were not offered because we came on a Tuesday.

We were not too impressed with the food at Busch Gardens, but if you can wait until Christmas Town opens at dark (6 pm) you have an opportunity to try some of the better food the park has to offer by purchasing one of the two food and beverage sampler packages available. The 8-option is $44.99 & the 12-option is $64.99. Just know that if you order a beer, it is served in what looks to be about 8oz cup, yet the food looks better than many other options we viewed/sampled throughout the day.

My two cents on the Nairobi Train Station's Serengeti Express: Ride the train during the day when you can actually see the animals. We went at nighttime thinking we would see a magnificent light show, but all you see is pitch black darkness until you get back. The best part of the whole ride was when everyone started howling like wolves once inside the tunnel.

Something to note: Elmo's Christmas Wish is the same storyline as the one at SeaWorld and is performed outside in the Sesame Street section of the park. The show is adorable, and the kids seem to love it. You can sit very close to the stage, and the characters will do a photo op with kids/adults after the show. There is also a large playground/jungle gym nearby for kids to play while waiting for the show to start. Nancy and I got in there and had so much fun!

Once again, some minor issues here, just like SeaWorld, but nothing I would consider a deal-breaker, especially since we got such a fantastic deal on our tickets. If you have never been to Busch Gardens or if you are looking for something fun to do in Tampa, I highly recommend a visit here; however, look around for deals online through Groupon or AAA.com.

original_text:
This review is based on my most recent experience at the park. When we got to the park, at the 2nd entrance (once you've paid and entered with your ticket) there is a sign that lists all the rollercoasters that are not not in service for the day. There was 4 listed. REALLY? Four... not counting Gazi. There are only 9 in total and technically 5 were not working?!?!

Now, I 100% get there are probably major reasons for them to be down that include safety but that just seems insane to me that they would still charge full price for less than half of the rollercoasters which is the only reason we were there and why most people are. original_text:
Complete rip-off/scam. I truly believe the team has this set-up in a way to charge you as much as possible with no remorse for their poor management. Much of what I'm writing has been echoed in numerous reviews, which I should have listened too before I went.

My family and I spent over $600 on three single day tickets with FastPass Plus add-ons, expecting to experience the park's attractions at their best. Unfortunately, our visit was marred by several issues that significantly impacted our experience.

Firstly, nearly half of the rides were closed for "annual maintenance," which was not disclosed anywhere on their websites or app. We confirmed this with a customer service representative at the park, who stated that the website and app were not updated to reflect the closures. She mentioned that the marketing team was working on having this issue resolved in the future. This lack of communication left us feeling misled and disappointed, especially after paying for tickets and add-ons with the expectation of enjoying all available rides. The app showed everything was operational, what a scam in itself.

Secondly, we encountered problems with the FastPass system. The FastPass lanes were not properly monitored, allowing guests to bypass the system by lane-hopping without any verification of their FastPass access. We only observed one ride (Rising Phoenix) where someone was actually monitoring the FastPass line and scanning passes. On our first attempt to ride, the ride was closed for about 45 minutes after waiting in line for some time. When we returned for a second attempt, our passes were scanned again, but no other rides had their lanes monitored. Additionally, we noticed that some rides had their FastPass lanes roped off in the middle of the day for no apparent reason, while guests continued to hop into the FastPass lane from the regular line.

This combination of ride closures, lack of FastPass monitoring, and inconsistent lane management was extremely frustrating and disappointing. I expected a much higher level of service and coordination, especially with how much money we spent.

It was disheartening to spend a significant amount of money and time and not receive the experience we anticipated. We live outside of the U.S., had we known that there were going to be unannounced closures, and disregard for add-on purchases to "enhance" our experience, we would not have spent the time and money into this location the way we did.

Also good luck trying to get ahold of anyone, they never responded to my e-mail when I informed them of the issue. I had to call after waiting 10 days for a response, in which they basically said there's nothing they can do about how the park manages their staff and advising people of what's open and when. They were also aware that they do not advertise what is closed and when. (Probably on purpose).

Here's some advice:
- Buy the tickets at the park if you decide to go, and call ahead even before you do that to see what's open.
- Don't bother buying fast-pass lanes as no one will be there to monitor it anyways, it'll save you almost 200 bucks per person and you can just skip the line.
- If someone is scanning, don't worry, they probably will only be at one. (if they scan your pass once, you can't get a refund, which is why we couldn't get a refund because they scanned our pass once at the only ride that was being manned and said "well your pass was scanned so we can't issue a refund even if no one else was monitoring the other lanes lanes".
- Bring your own food, most of the stands won't be open anyways and its extremely over priced.

original_text:
This is one of my favorite theme parks for a couple of reasons. Although it caters more to the rollercoaster enthusiasts, it has things for the little ones and the helpful kiddie wranglers (grandparents) while the rest of the gang is in line or riding a coaster.

Iron Gawzi is my absolute fave. What's not to love?!It's fast. It's high, and that first drop is a doozy. Definitely gets the blood pumping.

Sheikira comes a close second for similar reasons.

Tried to experience the Sarangeti Flyer, but it got shut down when we were one group away from us getting on. It got closed for inclement weather, which is unpredictable for how long the storm will take to pass. So we left our spot in line for something else.

This time around, Tigris was down multiple times, and we weren't able to ride it. The woman working the line there said it's been going down 4x/day.

There is a Sesame Street Land with kiddie rides for the little ones. Kid friendly foods. They even have a Chik Fil A in the park.

For the grandparents who don't want to ride the rollercoasters, there are nice shows they can partake for entertainment such as a cirque show, ice skating show, and live band performances. Also, it is a plus to get out of the hot Florida summer sun for some rest and cooling off.

Tips: Get to the park early. The parking opens 30 mins before the park opens.

Some rides don't have someone standing on the side you board. So pay attention and split up into the different lines.

If you're staying all day, get the all-day dining. You're not supposed to share it.. but.. let's just say that it easily feeds 2 people per band as you can grab a meal every 90 mins. Keep a timer on your phone so you know when you can get another meal. The 90 min timer also helps remind you to take a break and get out of the sun for a bit of rest btwn rides.

Check the weather! All the rides get shut down when inclement weather is in the area. It's hard to tell how long the rides will be down for.

The fireworks show is spectacular check for which nights they have it and go on that day.

Go make fun memories with your loved ones!

original_text:
Busch Gardens is still one of my favorite amusement parks. Unfortunately, they no longer have the abundance of animals I was used to seeing. On this trip for my birthday, (2/2/22), I enjoyed getting away and perusing Tampa and Miami to see what;s changing in my home state.

Florida is always growing and building and so was Busch Gardens to my amazement. Though many of the rides were closed (seasonally), I was able to see the new hybrid wooden/steel roller coaster, the Iron Gwazi. It's currently being tested out with park employees and opening in March 2022.

There is currently a new theme show called: "Busch Gardens, Mardi Gras" and there's was a package you can buy to sample a variety of "New Orleans" Cajun and Creole foods/beverages and beignets.

So, for my personal adventure, The Congo was the only ride I was brave enough to venture. Then there was Falcon's Fury, the only other ride besides The Congo my daughter got on (with her 25 year old scared self) . The entire time, she kept calling Jesus. I was like, "Call Him baby, He'll save you!" I was scared for her tho, that thing went too far in the air for me.

As for the rides that were closed for the season: the Montu, Sheirka, Stanley Falls, Skyride. They all looked awesome but probably only runs when there's an exuberant amount of guests. All other rides were open.

I loved being here this time of year because we didn't have long lines to wait in. We did a lot of walking, which was great but eventually got on the Serengeti train. This train takes you around the park where you can see the animals out in the open. It was pretty cool seeing those majestic creatures; however, I missed the flamingos and just couldn't find them.

I was; however, able to see the penguins and they made me smile with their happy feet self lol. While I was filming them in the water, one swam up to me and just stayed there paddling his lil feet staring at me. I'm like, oooooookay happy feet, time for me to go!

So, parking is $30 unless you're an annual pass member at a certain level and you park for free. Pass members are afforded some great perks and benefits and it's not very expensive. For example, you can get up to 6 free guest passes, unlimited admission, 20%-40% off merchandise, 10%-20% of food/beverages, free admission to year round events and so on.

Anyway, I'd like to see more exotic animals out there and more perks for non annual pass visitors....oh and I don't think they offer free beer. I know that was many moons ago.

I have been an annual pass holder least 6 years and have mostly enjoyed going to Busch Gardens. I don't ride many of the rides, unless we bring family along, but I love the animal aspect. The events such as Mardi Gras, Food and Wine and Christmas town are always fun events.

I understand that COVID has had a major impact on many businesses, and Busch Gardens has suffered also, but for the price you pay, it just isn't worth it anymore. Many of the rides are still closed, and in the afternoons the animals are often inside. I usually go early just so I can see the animals.

I went for the October annual pass holders perk of animal encounters and was extremely disappointed. Three animals...a turtle, an armadillo and a porcupine. None of these are ones which you can have an encounter. Two of the three handlers were very informative, but the last just stood there. I had expected so much more.

They have removed almost all of the hand sanitizing stations which were located everywhere throughout the Park. The last I heard, COVID is still a problem in our world and people need to take precautions so it doesn't keep spreading.

We are opting for Zoo tickets next year and likely won't be returning to Busch Gardens.

original_text:
I visited Busch Gardens in January and February during the Mardi Gras Celebration. The overall experience was okay. The rollercoasters are awesome and if you are a roller coaster you will love it! Favorite coasters: Iron Gwazi, Cheetah Hunt, and Sheikra I love the roller coasters and the sky ride!

The food selections are poor and the quality of the food is not good. My first visit I got the bbq sampler to split with my partner. The fries were cold which was a bummer! The bbq items like the rib had a lot of fat on it. For the price charged I was disappointed with the meal.

Mardi Gras is going on at Busch Gardens and I was excited to try the rum Bundt cake. When I arrived to xcursions there was an employee who found cupcakes that were two days old in the fridge. He was telling the other staff that they did not check to make sure old items were removed. Hence, I did not purchase a bundt cake due to not knowing if it was good or if it was fresh.

Overall, the park needs improvements. The park is dated and needs an facelift for sure. Some things just look old. Customer service is lacking and many employees do not smile or seem happy. Parking price is a bit excessive at 30 dollars. That is more expensive than Disney parking!

If you are local or a roller coasters lover I would check it out Busch Gardens otherwise I would skip the park.

original_text:
Went here for the first time ever this past Sunday. Was meeting some old friends to do "howloscream" so our tickets were for 5pm to midnight.
This is place could not be more different from Disney. The park is located in an undesirable area of Tampa, mostly consisting of chain restaurants, pawn shops and vape/smoke shops.
The guy at the gate where we paid for parking was without a doubt on something or at the very least, high. I hesitated giving him my credit card.

The patrons visiting the park were mainly goth-hoes, drunks and regular hoes, who might possibly be drunk.
EVERYONE is vaping weed or smoking cigarettes so the stench of weed is EVERYWHERE. And like hey, I get being 21 and wanting to get high but, come on guys, can't you just hotbox in your car and then come in to the park.

Oh yeah, and there was a dirty diaper on the floor of the bathroom stall I used. Awesome!
So like, there are some awesome thrill rides here, buuuut, the place is a shithole.

Some of the employees are nice though. Met some friendly people.
And I also caught a cold.

Would I go back? Depends on how badly I want to ride a roller coaster.
Based on the experience though, I'd likely just go to universal instead.
Everything in the park was overpriced on par if not more expensive than Disney except the quality was way inferior. The food is all pre made sitting under heat lamps. 12$ for a bottle of beer. When you consider how rundown the park feels, $12 feels insane.

The rides we were able to ride were quite fun and the employees that actually did their jobs were pretty cool.

Ultimately this place just felt a like a cash grab. Between the disproportionate amount of "gift shops" versus places to find food and then the oddly placed carnival games everywhere, it felt very third rate, and not to sound "uppity" but it was just kindof gross.

I don't even know where to start... this was my family's first and last time coming to this amusement park. We had a horrible experience from beginning to end. It was hilarious to see that the parking lot and park itself had just one entrance. With an expected ETA of 9:30, we finally made it into the park at 11:30. Yes that's right, 11:30. It took us an extra 58 minutes to get into the parking lot and a whole hour to go through the one security line.

We collectively paid $700 to ride ONE ride, Shaikra. 3 out of what, 10 of the big rides were closed for maintenance or technical difficulties. Other rides held wait times of up to 4-5 hours! Yes you can get a fast pass for an additional $190 PER PERSON! I wouldn't even pay that in Disney world. But I definitely wish we drove the extra hour from my place to Disney world for $20 more!

Busch gardens Tampa is loosing a lot of future customers! I'll be sure to let anyone coming to this area know that it is absolutely not worth your time or money. I hate that we had to spend $700+ to figure out this is the worst amusement park in America, but ya live and ya learn.

We purchased tickets in advance for three. We had some discounts but still we paid little less than $350 for 3 including $25 parking charge.
We spent about $55 for lunch. It was a lunch for 3. If you have hungry family, maybe paying $30 pass for each person might be a better option.

By late afternoon some of the temporarily closed rides opened up but some never did. At first we felt like we wanted a refund but because it wasn't so busy, kids were able to go on the favorite rides multiple times.

We stayed at the park until 5pm. It was a perfect fun day for 7 year old.

Solo traveler here! Found a Groupon deal for admission plus all day dining. Can't get much better than that!

On a Thursday, I arrived early to the park (prepaid $25 for parking) and they let us in right around 0950. I came with an agenda and I was able to ride all the big rides (except Kumba which was down for maintenance) in 2 1/2 hours!! I even rode a few of them twice!

Social distancing was enforced as they left seats open between parties. A few rides I got to ride all by myself literally. The ride with the longest wait was Cheetah hunt, but it was TOTALLY worth it!! Every other ride was pretty much a walk-on earlier in the day. Never had to wait more than about 5 minutes.

The animals are beautiful. The park is beautiful. I had a REALLY fun time and I'll for sure be back. Can't wait to conquer Iron Gwazi!

Wow! We had a such a great time here and I haven't been to an amusement park in about 10 years, and it was the same park!

So thrilling to be a part of the roller coaster action again. Except, I did get a bit frightened when we rode the Scorpion coaster. Specifically, it was after the loop and towards the end of the ride when you go on your side in a funnel-like movement. I was terrified for my life at this point and had to take a minute to calm myself back down to reality after the ride ended.

We also walked through the bird sanctuary and saw flamingos on our way to it in their own outdoor habitat. The birds flew00 freely in the sanctuary and you can feed them if your heart desires as they fly on you.

Ended up meeting with our friend Paige who works with the elephants in the park and she gave us a detailed description of their work with the elephants and how they develop relationships with them throughout the years. So interesting and appreciative of what they do day-to-day to take care of the elephants and their habitat.

Highly recommend stepping into the park if you're craving a thrill for the day in Tampa!

original_text:
Worst theme park management of all-time.
DO NOT BUY TICKETS THROUGH THE APP!! It's a trap and you will have to go to the ticket office regardless. Tickets on the app are not tickets, it is the receipt for your purchase and does nothing at all.
DO NOT BUY A MEAL PLAN! You cannot activate it at most places that accept it. Management thinks it's a fun game to make it a scavenger hunt type scenario where you run around the park trying to find a vendor where you can activate it and get what you paid for.
Do you like merchandise? Hopefully you are at least 300 lbs because those are the only sizes anywhere. They are using an automated system to restock, somehow they can't figure it out in 2025 because finding a medium is like finding a white whale.
Operations are so slow. I like that they let you see the dispatch screen so everyone on the ride can see it tick past 200 seconds every dispatch.
The food is somewhere between Purina and what you get at the food bank. The pretzel was actually good. Everything else was disgusting. Do you want to dip your fries in ketchup? I feel terrible updating my review to a 1 star from a 5 star, but I feel like I have to based on our experience on Saturday. There is definitely a safer way to operate during Covid and nearby theme parks are doing it right. (Rating drop was due to Covid safety precautions, park cleanliness, ride wait times, food line wait times, etc)

We made reservations well in advance and purchased passes for Saturday, 11/28/2020. We are aware that Covid is out there, but have felt safe during our Disney World visits and assumed that Busch Gardens would be taking their safety measures on their website seriously. We were wrong and disappointed. The park was packed. It was the second busiest we've seen in 20 years of being passholders. We saw probably 25%+ of people without masks and no staff was asking people to wear them.

Due to the park being so crowded, the rides that were open had 65 min wait to 140 min waits. (A lot of rides were closed while we were there, so call ahead).

All in all, we won't be returning anytime soon. The risk for us isn't worth it.

The rollercoasters were great. They were thrilling and pretty varied as to what type of ride they were.

However, the staff at the park were incredibly rude and had no sense of urgency. We ate lunch at the Nairobi BBQ place. We watched one of the employees behind the counter playing with her hair with her gloves on then touch the food with the same gloves on. The other staff members were barking at customers asking what they wanted to eat or acted as if the customer was an inconvenience to them.

The ride loading is incredibly slow and drawn out. We were in the heat of June with 90+ temperatures and many of the queue lines had poor ventilation and areas of full sun. This was absolutely brutal waiting for rides that at any other theme park would have had a significantly shorter wait time. Multiple rides were running only one car which made wait times multiply.

The food is below average theme park food and fortunately, with the all day dining, I didn't have to pay full price because it was incredibly expensive for what you get.

The bathrooms were dirty, there are only a few water filling bottle stations around the park.

I will skip this park and pay more to go to Disney or Universal in the future.

original_text:
This trip was a huge disappointment. As pass members, we love this park but with the current restrictions we really haven't ventured anywhere. Decided to try the park with reservations, hoping for a relaxed family experience. Boy, were we shocked to see the crowds. There was TOO MANY people and very few rides, shows, or stores operational. Not even the tram takes you to the entrance. Your dropped off down the road to begin the day of walking to nowhere.

The crowds were too heavy with some areas bottlenecking. Yes, employees are walking around with signs that say social distancing, but we had to remind folks to back up. The masks are on the whole time, which is okay if everyone kept their mask on, which they don't. We did see security telling folks to put their mask on and over their nose, as we walked among the crowds.

But the major blow was that all the shows were closed up, plenty of the rides were too. So what ended up happening in the rides that were open, you were looking at 90+ minutes wait. There was nowhere to go, with this many people and so many activities and shows not available. Plus, I hated not having a map, none are being given to guests, to help guide us along and better inform us of what was available. Just to spend the day walking around, to find that rides are closed or have extremely long crowded waits is not acceptable.

Paid for preferred parking for our family member's vehicle and when we got there they inform us that the lot was full. Then we had to stand in the customer service line for over an hour to be given a refund with Busch money. It takes them 30 to 60 days to issue you a credit card refund. That's awful, especially when it only took them a second to charge it!

Yes, seeing the one elephant was cute. Taking a one-way train ride after a 40 minute wait to see the other animals running free was cool too. But walking by and not spotting any of the lions, cheetahs, or monkeys was disappointing. Even the penguin tank had maybe 10 in there.

Busch could do better and they had in the past. This was a mistake and glad we had passes, for we have hope it can only get better. Feel sorry for all those families that paid the extreme one day ticket per person to have nothing at the end of the day, except perhaps a picture with a mask on by a lighted tree.

original_text:
We decided to go to Busch Gardens for my cousin ms 14th birthday. Our tickets were around $90, but with taxes and fees each ticket was about $115. We arrived close to the opening time, around 10:15 am. You have to pay for parking but since we were at a walking distance from the park, we walked to save up the parking fee.

My family and I went on a Friday morning, and surprisingly, there weren't many lines in any of the rides. We firstly started with the rollercoaster Cheetah Hunt, the wait was only five minutes! We then went to Montu and Cobra's Curse, other rollercoasters that also had no wait. By 11 AM, we had already got into three rides.

After this, my family wanted to ride Iron Gwazi, but I was a bit dizzy from the other rides and a bit scared to be honest. So while they rode that rollercoaster (which the wait said was around 45 mins but ended up being less than 30 mins), my sister and I decided to go to the Sesame's Street area. They had a lot of cute merchandise and it is ideal if you have little kids.

We then went to SheiKra, which honestly, was my favorite rollercoaster. It was really tall, fast, and a bit scary, but the ride was smooth and you were able to see all of Tampa from the top. After SheiKra, my family rode Tigris.

We then went to Chik fil A for lunch, which was good but a bit overpriced for what we normally get outside of the park. After eating lunch, we were too full to ride rollercoasters, so we decided to go on a water ride, Congo River Rapids. Be careful, you get REALLY wet!! so prepare!

After this, we rode Sandserpent, rode Cheetah Hunt again, and our day was done!

We were able to take a lot of advantage of the park and we were able to ride every roller coaster we wanted to. We were also able to see several animals throughout the trip, such as alligators, elephants, and penguins!!

Some tips to keep in mind: if possible, go on a weekday, we went on a Friday so the park was fairly empty compared to other times. Most rides had a wait time of 5 minutes, and the most we waited for a ride was 30 minutes. Another tip I would give is to buy the refillable souvenir cup to get your drinks. Even though it was a bit pricey ($20) compared to buying a singular drink, it's worth it. Finally, a crucial tip I would give to anyone bringing personal items to the park: but a belt bag or a bag that you could take on roller coasters if possible. We had to pay for lockers for several of the rides or have someone not ride so they could keep our items.

Overall, Busch Gardens was a really fun, exciting trip and I will come back again!!

Last weekend we had the opportunity to visit @howloscream at @buschgardens and we had a blast! The haunted houses were very well produced and can easily compete to those from @horrornightsorl

The only downside was that it felt more crowded and lines were longer as there is only 5 houses vs. 10 that @horrornightsorl offers. So a fast pass is highly recommended if you plan on visiting all the houses. Also, there were less food options outside of the traditional food venues which had very long lines as well.

Here is a TIP if you wish to save some time in food lines: stop by the the Giraffe Bar in the Serengeti Plain area which is on the 2nd floor. Drinks and food were pretty good there and lines were much shorter

Overall, @howloscream was super fun! Houses and rides were awesome. We would say rides are probably even better than @universalorlando if you like thrill roller-coasters. Food options are a bit more limited tho and lines are long, specially as we get closer to Halloween...so keep those things in mind before going Visit @orlandotravelfoodies for more reviews and tips of local attractions and restaurants!!!

Was on a trip in Florida but didn't want to deal with Disney and since we are mostly into roller coasters, this was a much better spot for us. Came on a Thursday in early March. Parking was easy, but was surprised they were only running one tram to the main gate (at half capacity due to COVID), so we ended up hoofing it.

Didn't take too long to get in since we pre-purchased tickets online. The park is a bit meandering and kind of tough to walk through but is also a big loop. We knew it was going to be a bit of an atypical experience because of COVID and came with an open mind. Like everyone probably does we started the day at Cheetah hunt and a 45 minute line. We were worried the rest of the day would be like this so from our phones bought the speed pass (which is more the day of than purchasing in advance). We ended up skipping this line, but the rest of the park had relatively minimal lines (as did Cheetah Hunt later in the day).

Much of walking though the part was a bit strange due to how little was open. They had some food and beverage stations, but through the whole park only three full restaurants were operating. Unfortunately we didn't realize this so ended up walking halfway around the park at lunch time.

Since I have family in the area, I'm sure we'll come back again one day, but likely not for a very long time.

original_text:
When we arrived at the park we went to get our tickets scanned and were told that our passes were not for park admission but only a "Fast Pass". We couldn't even gain entry to the park. Upon realizing this was were quite upset and asked for assistance. We spoke to a nice gentlemen who advised us of the error we made in purchasing the initial tickets online. He advised us that he'd "void" out our initial tickets that were purchased in error, and get us the tickets that we needed to both get into the park and also gain admission to the "Fast Pass". He said he'd be back in 15 minutes and true to his word he provided us with the correct tickets of which we paid another $420.31. He advised that he voided out the initial charges of $320.32 and that we should see a refund to our credit card within 5-7 days (receipt/proof enclosed).

I went to check my credit card statement last night and the initial charges were not refunded. I then called the customer service number (813-884-4386). In speaking with a representative there I was advise that the initial tickets were "used" that they could not refund our money. These tickets were never used as they were supposed to be voided out when our new tickets were issued. On two difference occasions I was told I'd have to physically go to Busch Gardens in Tampa and request a refund in person. Twice I told the representative that I was from out of town and that this wasn't an option. I went back and forth and once I realized that I wasn't getting anywhere I asked to speak with a Manager. Upon my request I was told that a Manager couldn't assist me either. Nevertheless I still asked to speak with Manager and twice was denied my request. Feeling neglected and clearly not being helped, I advised that I was recording our encounter and that if he didn't forward me to a Manager I'd file a formal complaint. At this point the representative reluctantly placed me on hold and advised me that he would forward me to a Manager. I sat on hold for 45 minutes but a Manager never took my call. Unfortunately I couldn't wait any longer as I had a Zoom work call at works and had to hang up. About week later I e-mailed this time (versus a call) and was told twice I was not allowed to speak to a Manager. I've NEVER been denied access to a Manager at any company in ,y entire life.

This entire ordeal and been the most disappointing experience I've ever had at an amusement park. The experience completed ruined our day. Even though we paid for a "Fast Pass" we were only able to go on TWO rides the entire day due to how crowded the park was. Each ride we waited for over an hour. When we tried to go on a third ride we were told the "Fast Pass" line was 60-75 minutes long and at that point we had enough and left the park both frustrated and in disgust. While I understand it was a holiday week, I am not clear on why Fast Passes were clearly oversold. Waiting over an hour for each ride is not my idea of expectation of paying extra money for a "Fast Pass".

I had a caricature drawn of my Gramma many years ago by a fella named Jared. It was a bit odd looking, but he was the only "artist" not drawing at the time, so I figured I'd give him a shot. I wheeled her over to the easel and he got to work and lemme tell ya--his drawing made no sense. It has all of these weird wobbly shapes all over it and it looked like he was doing scribble colors like a little kid would.

My Gramma seemed to like it, so I gave him my money and even bought a frame so she could hang it up.

Fast forward 9 years later and my Gramma is now the newest angel to pass through the pearly gates of heaven. We decided to display her drawing at her funeral right next to the casket. It's what she would've wanted. Well...lemme tell ya--that drawing made a lot of folks upset! They felt like the drawing was some sort of cruel joke that turned the funeral into a carnival. I had to hide it after most of the family complained. It was a sad sight to say the least.

Anyways...don't let Jared draw your Gramma, and if he does--keep that drawing in a dark room where no one can see it!

Busch Gardens Tampa is an excellent park to bring your family to. It's an hour from Orlando, but worth the drive.

They have some of the state's best coasters and are Sea World's land animal park. Iron Gwazi just opened recently and it is a great upgrade for a legendary attraction. I would dare say this is Florida's answer to Six Flags.

While they've become more lenient with their mask policy when it comes to COVID, they do offer hand sanitizer in plenty of spots throughout the park. If I lived closer, I'd be an Annual Passholder and that says a lot.

First time back to Busch Gardens in years and overall did not disappoint. We went in a weekday so it was as busy but not overly so which was nice. Parking was easy enough. There is a tram that will take you to the park from the lot. All of the coasters were fun especially iron gwazi and sheikra.
The park is set up a bit odd and can be confusing so recommend using the map.

Riding: if you are a rider they have lockers near each ride that you can put your stuff in. They close 10 dollars all day and you can move your stuff from locker to locker. If you travel a tad lighter you can wear a Fanny pack on most of the rides. Ladies, it does have to be a Fanny pack with plastic clips for it to be allowed.

The only thing that keeps this from being 5 star was the heat. While we expedited it to be hot, I mean it is Florida in June, they lacked enough fans. They had fans closer to when getting on the ride and were very spread out. A lot of amusement parks have misters as well near the fans when standing in line but Busch gardens did not have them. There is one part of the park with a mister tunnel however you couldn't even feel it.

Highly recommend going outside of summer months.

Fun family trip (hadn't travelled since 2018 for a family trip) and my older teens loved the rides, my husband and I loved the animal areas and gorgeous landscaped areas and shaded walkways with so much to see! Once 4 pm hit a tremendous influx of people seemed to be entering, guessing for the Christmas Town foods for purchase and the related festivities?
Plenty to stay busy with, lots of walking (wear your best comfy walking shoes)! Food prices are up everywhere and amusement park food and beverage prices are always high. We opted for the all day dining wristbands which was $44 per person, but once we saw water priced at about $4/bottle and all food prices hefty we knew the bands were the way to go since staying til close. There are certain eateries the meal bands work at so investigate that via the Busch Gardens app.
We'd all 4 say only eat at the Smokehouse place if getting pulled pork or the chicken. The ribs and the brisket were so extremely fatty it was a bust! They also threw all the meats from a sampler platter atop the waffle fries that come with the sampler therefore creating soggy, cold, bbq sauced waffle fries. We found the large indoor Dragon Fire Grill & pub for dinner and it offers several fast counter service places to choose from plus beverage stations. This worked well and was conveniently located and had tons of seating.
Ride lines were reasonable from opening time til about 3 or 4 pm when a huge influx of people seemed to enter the park leading to super crowded walkways, seating areas outdoors, and much longer lines for many coasters. A special Christmas Town list of festivities and foods began at 4 we think. The festive foods are exorbitantly priced and appeared to be mere samples. The park sells 12 samples of Christmas Town "specialties" at a separate price if $40 something per person. We hardly saw anyone partaking in this but it's there.

original_text: It was freakin AMAZING! We haven't been on vacation in years and we are so happy we chose Busch Gardens. The rides are absolutely, beyond a shadow of a doubt, some of the most incredible things you can do as a human being. Every coaster is surreal. Religious experience level. The animals are also amazing! We saw tigers, elephants, giraffes, hippos, and chimps up close in beautiful habitats. Most of the staff are really nice. Some are a little less enthusiastic but it's super hot out right now and they have to focus and be serious about the job which I felt like that's what they were doing. They do a decent job of keeping all the waiting areas covered and fanned. There's air conditioned areas and spots with mister fans but it's Florida and it gets hot. It feels like a tropical jungle, not miserable. They even had free sunscreen dispensers! The food is definitely pricey & is "theme park quality" if you know what I mean, but our tickets were incredibly affordable and the gift shops have really nice stuff that isn't insanely overpriced. We found some items for under $10. They have fancier stuff for more money, but it's good quality. They have name brand stuff like Guy Harvey. Parking was $35 but that was the only extra charge we were hit with. The park was really nice so I felt like the money I was spending was being used to actually maintain the place. I didn't feel like they robbed us like other theme parks... the hotels in the area really could be nicer, but downtown is close and has nicer hotels & food if that's what you're looking for. Overall, if you're a roller coaster fan, this place is paradise. Epic. Iron Gawazi Roller Coaster is just... wow. We were blown away. You have to experience it in your lifetime! No words do it justice.

original_text:
Cobra's Curse...more like Kumba's Curse. Over ten years ago I went to Howlscream at Busch Gardens and someone threw up while on the front row of Kumba and it caused a 30 minute clean up delay. I had yet to witness a delay at Busch Gardens over the years until today. My friends and I decided to wait a little longer to be front row on Kumba and lone behold, as we were about to get on the rollercoaster next, a girl threw up in the front row. What was more disturbing was how the team cleaned it up. They used a spray hose to spray it down with water which caused it all go to the floor where you exit the ride. They ran the rollercoaster cars twice to dry it off and then sprayed it clean. It took about 30 minutes and seemed like the crew was figuring it out as they went. It's concerning that they did not have gloves nor did they wash their hands after. Working healthcare myself, Busch Gardens should provide their staff with PPE for moments like this.
Other than "Kumba's curse", the Skyride, Cheetah Hunt was closed in the morning, and Kobra's Curse was closed. Being a Florida resident, visiting parks in the winter months seems like off season is the worst time to visit which is why the Christmas Town happens. I was also disappointed that the safari ride seems to be only something guests pay for now as an extra excursion.

original_text:
If you're for thrill rides, Busch Gardens will not disappoint.

We purchased a fun pass back in July when we visited & was able to use it once during our stay, which is good until the end of the year. We did this because we planned to come back during the holidays to use it again, so we were able to get 2 more uses out of it.

Parking is $30, unless you have some sort of annual pass where general parking is covered. You then get in line for a shuttle to bring you to the park from the parking lot, but honestly, sometimes just walking to the park without the shuttle will get you there even faster, especially when it's crowded & lines are long. They could use a few more of these shuttles to keep the lines & waits shorter.

Lines can be long & hectic, so best to plan accordingly. We didn't look into it, but I believe you can purchase some sort of fast queue to shorten or bypass the general line. Not sure how this works, or how much, but info should be listed on their site. I do know they have limited quantities on this. So if you're short on time, or just don't want to wait in the long lines, you may want to take advantage of this.

We've rode Cheetah Hunt, which is our favorite so far. We even got to ride it one evening where they did the fireworks show, so it was a great experience to ride this while fireworks were going in the background.

We've also rode Montu. For some reason, this ride is usually our first ride of the day & then we just make our way through the rest of the park. Tigris was also fun & we have tried to ride Cobra's curse 3x on all 3 different occasions, but either there is always maintenance going on, or the line was over 2 hrs.

Most of the times aren't always accurate. I feel like we get through the line faster than what is displayed.

We also enjoy disneyworld, but when it comes to thrill rides, I highly recommend Busch Gardens.

I heard Iron Gwazi is being built & should be open to ride sometime in March of 2022 & will be known as North America's tallest hybrid coaster & the fastest & steepest hybrid coaster in the WORLD!

original_text:
I was hoping to get more pictures but I was too busy riding the rides. The park is pretty large and unfortunately the full train line was down for maintenance as was the sky ride so I definitely got a workout. The pluses you can experience both animals and extreme rides all on one place. I had already been to the zoo but there are a few items here not at the zoo like lions. The cost as part of the Tampa city pass which I bought for $100 and included this as a place to visit is a great deal. Even if your paying full price it's no more than comparable parks. There are many great coasters. I rode all that were operational. I would note several are in the top ten globally in terms of number of inversions with two having 7. Also one coaster is in the top ten for longest track. The lines were non existent on this day which was another plus. Note I visited during the week on a Monday in October. They have plenty of lockers to put your valuables which is another reason I didn't get many pictures as these coasters I would advise putting the phone in the locker while riding.

There are several shows but we were focused on rides. With no lines we easily rode all coasters by 3 pm and took a 1 hr lunch break in the giant pizza hall. The food was ok while I had a pizza that tasted pretty good the mozzarella cheese sticks had sat under the heat lamp too long and had firmed back up. The cup for soda is pretty essential while it's around $15 you get free refills all day you buy it. The best part is that there is a coco cola freestyle machine that provided all the choices I like in soda.

The first ride Sheikra was amazing. It takes you up the incline and then suspends you briefly at the edge before the drop. It was very smooth and a good ride. I liked it and felt it was in the top coasters I've ridden. Also Montu felt like I was in a fighter jet with inversions leading into tight turns.

The park of course is beautifully landscaped. The only negative is the layout is very difficult to navigate there are several dead ends and a few areas where you'll backtrack to get to another area. They do have an app with gps to tell you where to go but it doesn't habe bathrooms on it. Like other parks the bathrooms will be well hidden and difficult to find so be warned try and go way before you need to as you may not be near one or you'll have trouble finding one in some parts of the park because they are well disguised to blend into the buildings.

original_text:
This was my first time visiting Busch Gardens and it did not disappoint. My fiancé and I arrived right at rope drop so we arrived at 9:30am and got let in 9:45 but they had the park still sectioned off until 10am. The rides are quite intense so I wouldn't recommend the rollercoasters for little kids but they do have a kid area for easy rides. Here are my tips and tricks:

1. Stay at a hotel close by and just uber there instead of parking since it was $10 for total Ubers of getting there and leaving.
2. I recommend the ice show since it was very entertaining and had amazing colors and ice dancing. Great for kids!
3. Bring a small bag if you and your group are planning to go on the big rides since you have to put your items in the lockers. The lockers are quite small. I would recommend the $10 all day pass cuz you can switch different lockers throughout the day when you're going to different rides.
4. Bring a water bottle since you can refill it with the water fountains instead of buying their expensive water.
5. Go on the train if you need to take a break and you can see some animals during the tour but not as much as you would on the safari tour. *The safari tour is about $80-$100.*

Rides on order to visit:
1. Iron Gwiza - one of the best rides of the park! It opened about a year ago so it is one of the newer rides at the park.
2. Montu - if you like going upside down this is the ride for you! Your feet does hang so wear tennis shoes otherwise you can set your sandals or flip flops to the side.
3. Sheikra - this one of my favorite rides! You do drop about 20 ft and you do not get wet but you do go through water. This ride can hold about 12 people so they do go faster with the line.
4. Kumba - similar to Montu where you do go upside down! I did get nauseous after the ride.
5. Cheetah Run - Honestly based on TikTok this ride was hyped up and said it was the one to go to right away. I didn't think it was that great as it wasn't the most intense as Montu and Iron Gwiza. It honestly just goes really fast is what makes it unique.
6. River ride - You will get wet on this ride so bring an extra set of clothes and wear flip flops! It is really fun!

I loved the coasters, I loved how low the wait times were. Cheetah Hunt is my favorite ride of all time! But there was a particular experience that soured things for me:

"Can I have a water?" I asked the Village Snacks employee. She started filling up a water cup for me. 

"Oh, no, sorry- a bottled water," I said.

This large woman with a huge mole was extremely rude for literally no reason at all. "That would've been free," she said sarcastically.

"I wanted to be able to close the cap," I muttered. I was going to be carrying the water around the park; it was much easier to have a bottle, not a soda cup. She made me feel like I was weird for asking for a bottle. She continued to be rude, asking "which one?" and getting mad that I didn't immediately understand that she was asking what brand of water I wanted. "Which one?!" She said again, annoyed. She then got irritable again when I didn't put my card in the right spot right away. 

This was 10:45AM on Monday, August 19, 2024, at "Village Snacks" by the entrance. It is unfortunate that this nasty individual put a bad taste in my mouth first thing in the morning, because every other park employee I encountered was very kind the rest of the day. Perhaps don't put your mean, nasty, rude for no reason employees in the front of the park.

original_text:
Don't know why this place has bad reviews.

Everything was super fast for us... parking, tram, getting in etc. we showed up as the park opened because I read the bad reviews about waiting and we didn't need to. I was stressing for nothing.

If you don't want to wait in line, get the quick queues. You're in and out of a ride in a few minutes. If you don't, there were still people who walked on the ride and off the same exact time as us and we had the quick queues.

If you go early, there's very few there! We went as soon as it opened up and everyone pretty much walked in the rides.. the later you go, the more people.

The rides and stores open up throughout the day. If you make it a day trip, you'll see it come alive.

There are a few attractions closed but I spent a full day there and was very satisfied. The food and drinks are expensive but there's also a McDonald's like across the street.. eat a quick meal before coming in. Then buy some snacks or a meal before you leave.

The shows are awesome. The animals were cool to see and we enjoyed the rides!

Bring bug spray !

original_text:
Tampa's African Safari amusement park! Whenever I've came to Busch Gardens, it's mainly to see the animals. I would go as far as to say that they're not known for their rides. Don't get me wrong, they have some pretty fun rollercoasters, such as the Sheikra( My favorite) and Montu, but the main attraction are all the animal exhibits and tours.

We decided to go on the Serengeti Safari Tour! Whew I had a BLAST. We saw all types of animals, but being as giraffes are one of my favorite animals, I jumped at the chance to feed one. They were so calm and sweet. I honestly don't think they gave a damn about us being there, they were more so interested in the wedges of lettuce the Safari Instructor handed to us to feed them. They also had a cameraman on our truck to take out photos which were available for ridiculously high prices after the tour.

One thing I will say is that although I always have a blast here, it sure will cost you a pretty for just about everything. In my opinion, the only way to make Busch Gardens worth it is to buy the a citypass and visit multiple places in addition to Busch gardens because it costs most as much to get into the park.

Also the tours can really add up in price as the safari tour was an extra $40 per person and you had to reserve your seats days in advance!

Seems like I'm talking about money a lot but I just wouldn't want anyone to come here expecting a deal on anything haha. Otherwise it's fun for just about anyone regardless of age. I'd definitely come back if I were to visit Tampa again!

original_text:
A couple of months before the end of 2024, I asked my kids what they wanted to do for Christmas. They told me that they wanted to go to Busch Gardens. Of course my kids wanted to go on the rides ESPECIALLY on the new Iron Gwazi. I was fortunate to be able to get a package deal to go to Sea World as well. In addition, I paid the meal package for the four of us for both Sea World and Busch Gardens.

So, I paid well over $1000 between airfares, hotels, parking, gas, entrance fees, food, etc. to make that happen. We didn't have any major issues with Sea World. However, our experience at Busch Gardens was a nightmare. Since I am a senior citizen, there are limited rides I can handle. The one I really wanted to go on was the Congo River Rapids. Both the Kumba and the Congo River Rapids were closed. In fact, other rides were shut down temporarily or permanently for the day. Three times my son waited on line for at least 30 minutes for a particular ride and then it was shut down by the time he got to the front of the line. My partner and I primarily came to see the shows. You had to get there 30 minutes early to be able to get in. By the time a show was over, you had to walk many times 20 minutes or more to go to the exact opposite side of the park to see the next show, so, by the time you got there, it was completely full and you couldn't get in.

My daughter and I are vegetarian. Prior to going to Busch Gardens, I studied the menus to know the places we could get salads or other vegetarian items. The wait to get most food was an average of 30 minutes and then once we got to the place to finally order, they were out of salads or whatever else we were able to eat. Then, it was tough to get a table to sit down. Once we had to eat standing up.

I noticed that one of your restaurants offered funnel cake. I was so looking forward to having one. It would be considered a full meal ticket, but, that was fine with me. We waited 40 minutes and when we finally were able to order, we were told that we could not get a funnel cake because it was considered a dessert. We would have to order chicken fingers or whatever non-vegetarian meal they offered....nothing of which we could eat.

But, the real kicker was this....The main goal of going to Busch Gardens was for my kids to go on the Iron Gwazi. When my kids arrived at the line and read the sign stating there would be a 2 plus hour wait, one of the Ambassadors in charge of the line was telling everyone to come back in the evening because the line would be much shorter then. So, that's what my kids did. However, when they came back to finally get on the ride, the line closed down 2 hours early because the park was having fireworks that night. So, they never got on the one ride I came all the way from Arizona to have them ride.

There was no sign earlier in the day stating it would close early and it was THEIR Ambassador who told them to come back later.

So, this entire experience was a very frustrating, horrible experience. I sent this email to them 3 times and no one ever bothered to respond.

Busch Gardens, you REALLY need to up your game! Yuck! Not worth the money!

Busch gardens has a few safety measures in place for the pandemic. Masks are required by everyone two years and up. And they somehow take everyone's temperature by having us walk in a tent--Cool technology.

The self service ticket lines are a little long because every other one is closed for social distancing, so arrive early if you need to get tickets printed.

This is the first amusement park/attraction I've been to since Covid, so I'm not sure what other places are doing, but I was hoping to see rides and trams sanitized after each use. Or maybe even someone giving hand sanitizer to the guests before they got on the ride to keep it as clean as possible.

I wish there were more shows available, our only options were music and Sesame Street on a Saturday. But we mostly came to see animas with the kiddos on this trip, so they were happy.

The food we had was sub par with a hefty price tag, like other theme parks. We all had chicken strips or chicken sandwiches. The chicken was dry, and the fries were not warm or flavorful. Definitely take advantage of the Food and Wine options if you can, we will be trying that out next time!

original_text:
Busch Gardens is a unique amusement park, essentially a Six Flags and a zoo put together. This hodge podge at times seems a bit strange, but I enjoyed the variety - and hopefully the diversity of attractions means there's something for everyone in your party!

Many of the roller coasters are a bit short and may have long waits. If you're going during a busy season, a fast pass will likely be a wise investment. Though I came in December, so it was pretty empty. The animal attractions are pretty cool, with some of my favorites being the kangaroos, the interactive bird houses, and the big cats. You can also take the train through the large plains to see more animals that aren't as easy to see from the pathways.

Pricing can be pretty steep - as much as $100 per person or more! But if you get the Tampa Bay City Pass, that can make it more affordable (as long as you also want to do other bay attractions). Also, while the park itself is nice, the area it's in is a bit run down, so be aware of that if you're booking lodging or finding parking (it's best to pay for the park parking, even if it's pretty steep). Overall a fun experience if you're a fan of thrill rides and wild animals.

We have lived in the Tampa area for more than 40 years and have had passes of some type many of those years. I bough my son and his girlfriend gold passes, which come with additional 1 day passes, so my husband and I went with them today. While the passes were a pretty good deal, and I enjoyed the beautiful weather there, I was somewhat disappointed in the park. The train is not working still, due to hurricane Milton. Many things, such as the sky ride, the skating show, some eateries, don't open until 2 pm. The sky ride costs extra for non pass holders. Some animals were not seen, such as the tiger, which has a whole exhibit area and gift shop
The plants, green areas and decorations were disappointingly sparse. The children's area looked like the hurricane happened a week ago; the jumping place was not inflated, full of water and leaves.
So, while we enjoyed a day of cool weather, and low park attendance, with family, it just isn't the same great quality park that it used to be. If the park is open, a large majority of attractions should be open also. Step it up Busch Gardens, not everyone wants to spend the day on roller coasters. Probably won't renew those passes.

original_text:
Busch Gardens Tampa was an awesome experience! From the food, to the animals and roller coasters to the customer service....it was a great day!

When me and my party arrived to the park it was storming. I was surprised at the number of people that were at the park. We didn't have to pay the $22, for parking which started our day off nicely.

I would highly recommend purchasing the meal plan with your ticket as it is worth the extra money! You can eat a full meal every hour if you like. Plus water is free and snacks are buy one get one free, which included most everything even beer.

We started off with a meal upon arrival since it was still raining. We took advantage of the rain and hit up all the animal exhibits the first few hours. Then once it stopped raining and there was no lightning for a certain period of time we were able to start riding the roller coasters.

I had no idea Busch Gardens had such awesome roller coasters. We road them all, and two of the best coasters we rode again after dark. We saw a spectacular fireworks display around 9:30 PM. And we stopped by a snack bar on the way out and got our last free water and I purchased some caramel corn at buy one get one free.

The shuttle was nice too so you didn't have to waste your energy walking from the parking lot to the park. When the park closed the shuttles were coming every 1 to 2 minutes which was great! We even received a free ticket for another day within a year due to the inclement weather. What an awesome deal!

Thank you Busch Gardens for making my first experience at your amusement park GREAT!

original_text:
Amusement park or abusement park ?
I have been going to Busch Gardens since the early 1970's and never been disappointed. Well all that changed on Sunday. We decided to visit the park as a quick break before the wife , a teacher, had to go back to work.
We bought our tickets on line including the meal plan. We were shocked when we got to the parking window and it was $30.00 to park. That was the beginning of a terrible day.
We entered the park, waited in line to get our dining wristband and then went to enjoy some rides. The first one we passed was the Iron Gwazi which we had not ridden before, it was not running . Next we came upon the Stanley Falls flume ride , also closed. We kept walking and arrived at SheiKra where there was a 30 min wait. We went to get in line and were sent to put our bag in a locker $3.00 . Walked back to the ride and it was now showing a 45 minute wait. Started in line and there was a constant flow of people with fast passes boarding the coasters faster than they could get the " regular " customers on the ride. We watched the only two coasters fill with fast pass people only , before we were lucky enough to get on the ride. The total wait time was over an hour.
We had been in the park for almost two hours and were only able to board one ride . This was ridiculous and we went up to the customer service line to request a refund. We spoke to Veronica , who said she was the regional manager and were told that since we entered the park gate we had used our ticket and all she would do was offer us another day to come back.
We decided to stay since we had already made the drive and had no intention at this point to ever come back.
At the entry before you get in the park there was a sign listing rides that were closed and wait times, it was blank. The rides that were running were only using half of the lines and the wait times were crazy. A few of the attractions that used to be included in the admission price are now " experiences " and require an additional fee.
We decided to eat and then go to the zoo part of the park. Lunch was a meatball sub and was not worth the time it took to get it. Only half the food lines were open and the wait was long. The zoo continued to disappoint with animals that didn't look healthy and employees that definitely were not happy. Lots of animal habitats were closed as well. There was one poor elephant that didn't even eat the fresh treats being thrown to it by an employee.
Florida is having record high temperatures and the wait times were brutal. Many of the air conditioners in waiting areas were broken as well. We went to the smokehouse to eat and the line was long and we were inside a building with no air . Food was warm , fries were inedible and the staff was miserable. The vents over the food line were rusted and covered with dust and grime. The entire park is in disrepair and needs to be cleaned and updated.
My parents brought me here when I was a teenager , and I brought my children here as well . It saddens me that my children will not continue the tradition due to the terrible condition of the park in general.

Being born and raised in Florida I've never came to Busch Gardens before and it was until I saw their BOGO deal that it was a sign to finally visit. Tickets are usually $80 + tax. So the BOGO deal was definitely worth the price.I came on a Sunday morning at around 11am (park opens at 10) and parking was pretty busy especially since there is traffic coming from both sides of the street. After parking you can get on a tram to get to the front of the theme park. The park has a mixture of roller coasters, mini rides from younger children, and zoo spread out throughout the park. There are animals such as tigers, lions, cheetahs, orangutans, rhinos, penguins, and more! It's cool to walk from different roller coasters and see these animals casually living their lives. The rides had decent waiting times and you definitely get an adrenaline rush after riding them.

On this day, it did end up raining around 3pm that lasted 2 hours causing the rides to close down for safety reasons. My group was concerned because we came to Tampa for this one day and it was being wasted by the rain, but! We were in line for guest relations and BG has a rain policy which meant they gave us tickets that we can use on another day!! I can't wait to go back to ride the coasters that I didn't get to go on due to the rain n

original_text: I have been to quite a few amusement parks in my time. Over the years they all seemed to have gotten away from leaving people with a smile on their face and more towards how much money they can bilk you out of. This is the first time I was provided free tickets as a veteran, which was great, but again one hand giveth and the other hand taketh away. They slap you with a hefty fee just to park ($35), but do have a tram to take you to the front gate, that is if you want to wait an extra 10-20 minutes in line instead of walking the short distance to the gate. Our first coaster was the Iron Gwazi. A coaster I would not recommend for the faint of heart. I resigned myself to be dead by the end of the ride and kept my eyes closed for most of it to maintain my ability to keep my lunch down. This was bad, but not as terrifying as the giant group of wasps at the top of the first peak. The other coasters were good, but one had us wait an extra twenty-five minutes due to technical difficulties and this coaster had the shortest wait time, which cancelled that out. They had a couple places where you could watch the wildlife without paying for the encounter experiences, which seemed to be more of a rip off than anything. I mean, paying to stand in the back of a truck with no canopy on a safari. I am partially disabled and this is not acceptable. All over the park there were signs indicating you would be ejected if caught cutting in line, but when you inform security, they do nothing-what a joke! After a good amount of rides we decided to eat at Zagora Cafe'. It cost us $115, which included a service charge for three bacon cheeseburgers with fries, three drinks, one water, two cookies, a parfait and a small cup of grapes. Now that is a rip off. Yes, you can by the meal plan, which is only good for the meal, but not any snacks and you only break even if you eat twice, which is only a benefit if you can keep your lunch down after a coaster. All-in-all I would not recommend this park as there are plenty of other sites to see when visiting Tampa.

original_text: Busch Gardens is one of those Tampa Bay staples that every local should be going to at least every other year or so. This theme park is more than just rides: they're a venue which hosts all kinds of events (food and wine festival), they're a zoo, and they're a garden. There's also alot of education they provide about the animals and plants they host. Now I do have one disclaimer which is that I didn't go on any rides or roller coasters besides the train and the carousel, but this is a testimony that there-s something for everyone, whether you're a thrill-seeker, adrenaline junky, or just wanna get away from everything. Perfect place to go for holidays and special events. I myself come every year for their Howl-O-Scream event ALMOST every year but one. This is my second time coming out to their Christmas Town where everything is decked out for the holidays. We spent seven hours at the park, and in those seven hours we did alot that was not roller coasters or rides. We went on the train and saw the animals: antelope, giraffes, rhinos, and zebras wandering and roaming freely. Then we went on the other exhibitions and saw the chimps and even had a chance to go underneath a tiger in his enclosure. We had a chance to pet baby kangaroos and be surrounded by birds. The food here is good, but menu is limited and pretty pricey. I highly recommend the smoked chicken, which was large, fall off the bone, and full of smoke flavor. It really hits the spot on a warm day with sone coleslaw and waffle fries.

original_text:
Ever been a bit more than pleasantly surprised? I am vacationing with teen boys... yes, the hard to please demographic. Ugh! aww man! I dragged them out of bed this morning, hustled them to get into the car. Sped to the complimentary bus pick up point (bus almost left us - note the bus was pulling out exactly ON TIME!). The dreaded looks I got on the hour ride from Orlando.

So you see five stars right? Those skeptics I dragged out of bed this morning had the time of their lives!

The assumption was "gardens and animals with one or 2 mediocre rides." Now they can't say one bad thing. The thrills of the rollercoasters, the shows, the train ride through the mini safari, the food, the deserts (the deserts were GONE before I could get a pic), no complaints.

So I would STRONGLY advise first time visitors like myself to get there for the 10am park opening and head to Cheetah Hunt rollercoaster FIRST. We got there at about 10:45 and the wait was already 50 minutes. Next, the Cobra's Curse - that was 45 minutes. All other rides were 15 to 30 sometimes less. I was told by a regular, those lengthy wait times happen because those rides are the first to open. So best to get there early, get them done, then cruise through the rest of the park.

The event/shows schedule can be picked up with the nap at the front gate. Super easy to plan your entire day so that you see all you want to see and do all you want to do.

Rides for all age groups. Exciting atmosphere. And the lights at night are beautiful.

Truly a fun day with teenage boys! Will return next year same time with the entire family!

original_text:
Busch Gardens Theme Park of Tampa Bay features plenty to do for young kids, older kids, teenagers, adults, and even grandparents! Adults and older kids can ride Rollercoasters, adults and grandparents can watch shows, and young kids can enjoy all the rides at Sesame Street Safari of Fun! I've been going to Busch Gardens years, ever since I was a little kid! I liked Busch Gardens better when Anheuser-Busch was operating it and gave free beer, fireworks, and snacks. Busch Gardens was also better in 2018 but it's also maybe unfair to judge the park now with reduced capacity, covid restrictions, and the unemployment benefits crisis due to people being paid more not to work than to work.

Festivals include:
Mardi Gras 8/10
Food & Wine Festival 8/10
Summer Nights 6/10
Bier Fest 7/10
Howl O Scream 8/10
Sesame Street Kids Weekends 8/10
Christmas Town 10/10

Rides Include:
1. Tigris Rollercoaster 8/10
2. Cheetah Hunt Rollercoaster 10/10
3. Cobra's Curse Rollercoaster 10/10

6. Montu Rollercoaster 8/10

9. Stanley Falls Flume Water Ride 6/10

12. Sand Serpent 7/10

14. Wild Surge Kids Drop Tower 8/10
15. Sky Ride 9/10

Animals Exhibitted include: Cheetahs, Tigers, Lions Rhinos, Sloths, Gorillas, Orangutans, Chimpanzees, Elephants, Giraffes, Hippos, Hyenas, Penguins, Meerkats, kangaroos, macropods, alligators, snakes, giant tortoises, komodo dragons, gazelles, antilopes, Zebras, flamingos, peacocks, bald eagles, tons of different birds, and more!

Kids Rides Include:
1. Air Grover roller coaster 9/10
2. Elmo's Safari Go-Round Carousel 7/10
3. Rosita's Djembe Fly Away 5/10
4. The Count's Zambezi Car Rally 9/10
5. Big Bird's Whirly Birdie Ferris Wheel 5/10
6. Snuffy's Elephant Romp flying Carousel 9/10
7. Zoe-Patra & the Hippos of the Nile water ride 9/10
8. Bert & Ernie's Watering Hole 10/10
9. Telly's Jungle Jam Obstacle Course 7/10
10. Elmo's Treehouse Trek 10/10
11. Cookie Monster Canopy Crawl 10/10
12. Smiley's Sahara Sands Giant Sand Box 8/10
13. Cookie Monster Cloud Bounce 9/10
14. Gwazi Flyers Flying Carousel 8/10
15. Ubananga Banga Bumper Cars 9/10
16. Busch Flyers Flying Carousel 8/10
17. Pantopia Carousel 5/10

Live shows include:
1. Sesame Street's Let's Play Together 6/10
2. Turn it Up Ice Skating Show 8/10
3. Cirque Electric Acrobatics Show 9/10
4. Spark! Fireworks Show 6/10
5. Critters Inn Charge 9/10
6. Rock a Doo Wop 9/10

Busch Gardens Themed Areas include:
1. Morocco 8/10
2. Sesame Street Safari of Fun 8/10
3. Bird Gardens 6/10
4. Stanleyville 7/10
5. Jungala 8/10
6. Congo 4/10
7. Pantopia 6/10
8. Nairobi 4/10
9. Serengeti Plain 6/10
10. Edge of Africa 8/10
11. Egypt 9/10

Favorite Past Rides include:
1. Tanganyika Tidal Wave Water Ride 10/10
2. Gwazi Wooden Rollercoaster 9/10
3. Questor 4D 9/10
4. Akbar's Adventure Tours 4D 10/10
5. Rhino Rally 8/10
6. Monstrous Mamba 8/10
7. Sandstorm 9/10
8. Jungle Flyers 10/10

While Universal's Islands of Adventure is better for older kids and adults, Busch Gardens really offers a lot more rides for younger kids. Busch Garden's Sesame Street Safari of Fun is much bigger than Dr Seus Land at Universal. Most of Disney's Magic Kingdom is pretty young kid friendly with exception of their Rollercoasters just like Busch Gardens also has the added benefit of lots of animals on display kinda like Disney's Animal Kingdom! Universal and Disney has Busch Gardens beat for innovative food selections, movie tie-in, ride quality, theming and overrall feel. Busch Gardens food selections need a lot of work as most places just have subpar overpriced burgers, Pizza, and hot dogs aside from the seasonal festival foods. However, Disney & Universal theme parks are comparatively much more expensive for annual passes but only a little more for a one time pass. Busch Gardens let's you come back all year with a fun pass for the price of a 1 day admission! Unfortunately, Busch Gardens is way too spread out and it requires way too much walking in comparison to other theme parks in Orlando.

I really hope Busch Gardens management can bring back more live shows at the Pantopia Theater and Pantopia Dragon Fire Theater, bring more popular concerts, offer a greater variety of foods at restaurants, offer a greater variety of foods at festivals, and offer more types of rides than just Rollercoasters like 4D Rides! If managment could duplicate all the international food offerings at the superior International Food & Wine fest of Busch Gardens Williamsburg VA that would be awesome!

original_text: My family visited Busch Gardens Tampa on February 26 during a week-long Florida vacation...grandparents, parents, and a 2-1/2 year old. My daughter had downloaded the BG app prior to vacation, and had our whole day planned out. I had purchased tickets ahead of time, through AAA, that included all-day dine. First of all, there was no signage directing us to parking. My son-in-law actually drove past the parking lot and had to turn around. Once we got in the park and started walking around, we found that many of the attractions my daughter had planned, were closed. The train station we stopped at was closed, and we didn't know, until much later, that there were two others (of which one was also closed). Signage is terrible. Many eating establishments did not accept all-day dine, and it was difficult finding ones that did. It was a long, hot day with a lot of walking, trying to find something that was actually open that we could enjoy. Apparently there was a Mardi Gras celebration on Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays...and we were there on a Wednesday...but still...even the little kiosks were closed! I paid $542.80 for the tickets and I want a refund. I called AAA...they were interested in what I had to say, but said I needed to contact Busch Gardens directly with my complaint. I have now contacted Busch Gardens five times...twice on-line, and three times via phone...and have had no resolution. The on-line methods were via links on their website...but nothing for "Guest Services." The phone call has an automated menu, but again, no "press __" for Guest Services." One phone-call representative told me I needed to return to the park and talk with Guest Services. Well, I'm in New Hampshire, so that's not going to happen! Very disappointed with our visit, and with the lack of customer service.
