original_text:
Took three grandkids to Busch Gardens this week, spring break! Best decision ever! We bought the fun passes for about 125 each. But we can use it until December. I use a mobility device and found the park to be very accessible. Even their train ride has a lift so I was able to take the chair right onto the train. It was a perfect mix of thrill rides for the older grandkids and laid-back Safari and animal viewings and gardens for me and the younger one. There was even an amazing Sesame Street land with ball crawls and trees to climb etc.!
The older grandkids did a couple thrill rides but the lines were really long. I learned about their version of fast pass too late to get them for them. So I would recommend if you want to do the more popular rides, check into their version of fast pass. It will cost you but everything else cost you too so...
It happens that they are doing their food and wine festival. Beautiful gardens lovely variety of foods, wines , Bourbons whiskeys and desserts.
My only concern was with the accessible parking. They sell part of the accessible parking lot for preferred parking. Unfortunately for those of us who don't have a choice, if you don't get there early, there is no accessible parking. As I explained to the attendants I really don't have a choice but to use accessible parking as the grandchildren aren't old enough to drive to drop me off and I can't get my chair on their tram. So I just kind of pushed ahead and went into the "full" accessible parking lot. I found a space that wasn't really a space but I made it into one, two days in a row! Freaked the grandkids out that I was parking illegally.lol but I like to keep them on their toes! I promised them we would not get arrested for parking illegally with a handicap parking and my mobility scooter! Would not be good for business to have that on the news! Lol.
So go while it's still tolerable weather! They have a lot of lovely gardens and shaded areas. If you go don't miss the food and wine festival!

Went today with some friend to spend our afternoon doing something fun. Today was the first time riding Tigris and cheetah hunt and I can say that both rides are for people looking for that high but can not do larger coasters that have to many loops or goes to high. The first thing that gets your blood moving with both rides is the acceleration using magnets to get both rides upwards speeds of 45 for Tigris and 65 MPH for cheetah hunt.

One highlight for each ride that makes them both so unique is that cheetah hunt towards the end, zig zags like how cheetahs would run after their prey and with Tigris having multiple twists through out the ride to give you that "big coaster loop" feel.

The wait time for cheetah hunt as of today was only at 5 minutes since they have two coaster cars verses the 20 minutes you can expect for Tigris with it only having one coaster car. Both rides regardless of wait times are both worth the wait.

10/10 perfect for a fun family night out weather you are a local or a vacationer.

original_text:
I don't even know what to say.
My family purchased a 3-park pass and decided to check out Busch Gardens on the 2nd day. This is the park that my husband has been waiting to go to forever... and I was so happy to be able to take him.

So getting there wasn't bad and the parking wasn't the greatest - since we had to park on the other side of the park where you have to take a tram to get to the entrance of the park - but we made the best of it.

We get thru security and showing our tickets to get in and we thought that the adventure was going to begin... like at Sea World... but low-and-behold, we were so surprised to find that you have to walk so much before you even see anything! What?!?

We see a ride coming up and notice that it's not running... come to find out that it's not even finished yet! Ugh!

More walking it is...

So we come upon some areas that have some beautiful flowers and topiary... loved it. But no more... we had to walk even more to find an area with the kangaroo and further down, some birds.

Where the heck are the rides in this joint?!?!
Found Sesame Street area found... our daughter got in one ride there and we walked out.

More walking and we finally find some rides but at this point - almost an hour in and we hadn't found a concession stand to get anything to drink and mind you, it's like 90° while walking! There were about 5/6 concession stands and restaurants that we passed that were just closed.

So we had to stop to get a drink at the first concession stand we found that when we got in line, only one of the registers were being used and the line was super long!
We finally got our drinks and snacks, ate them and decided to jump into the line of the first water ride we see. Score!

No score...
After waiting over an hour and a half, the employees come over the PA system to tell us that the ride was shutting down because it was broken but we were more than welcome to stand in line until it was fixed! What?!?! Why would I continue staying in line for a ride that was completely shut down?!

So we get out of line and were just disappointed with the whole experience. There were barely employees around to help with answering questions, which was very strange to see.

The layout of the park was so strange... I don't know how to explain it but it wasn't easy to read the maps that they have in order to get around.

So we decided to leave the park - and thank goodness we did when we did because a storm came ripping thru the park - and we got to the area where you get on the tram and come to find out that the park had stopped the running of the tram because of the storm that was coming. And they weren't going to start them up again for 45 minutes! What?!?! You bring your patrons from across another parking area that isn't close by on your tram and now we have to walk to the other side - thru a long tunnel and cars whizzing by and falling branches from palm trees, no less! Busch Gardens... do you not care about the safety of your patrons!!!???

So... that's the end of our adventure at Busch Gardens. I felt that it was a waste of time and money... even though the hubby says he'll give it another try one day - maybe never for me.

original_text:
Such a day filled with fun in the sun!!! I went by myself and surprisingly I had so much fun. I arrived at opening time and got super discouraged because of the super long lines outside of the park. Shortly after I realized it was the Covid-19 prescreening. It did feel like forever but taking how many people were in line (sesame Street opening weekend) it wasn't that long. I was super happy to know the park is taking so many precautions also. The customer service was amazing and the employees were super friendly. This is a great place to have fun and enjoy some educational learning! It's literally a zoo and theme park all in one. I purchased the food package with my entry ticket, and upon arrival I purchased the skip line pass and It was worth every penny. I finished the rides so fast (I rode every ride once) and did the train ride and walking tours in no time! So fast that I didn't even use my food pass! This started as an exciting trip to Busch Gardens in Tampa. I'm not from Florida but I have traveled the world and have visited many theme parks.
Today, 23 November 2021, my husband, son, mother-in-law and niece started at Busch Gardens around 2 pm and we bought the express pass ($69.00 each) to skip all of the ride lines because it's my husband and My vacation before we both start our next military duty station. We started off in the Emporium and bought super cute refillable cups. As we walked the entire park, we found that all of the rides were "temporarily closed" except the ride Mantu, to our knowledge. All of the refillable stations were closed and the one we did find had a long line and didn't open until 4 pm. The three disrespectful girls that were working at this beverage stand next to the swan garden sculpture and Iron Gwazi (which didn't open until March 2022) have no customer service skills. My husband approached the stand and asked if they refilled the water cups there and with attitude one of the girls shoot her head no. He shook it off and went back into the line with my mother-in-law. Finally it's there turn, they approach the counter once more my mother-in-law asks "What products do you have?" The girl behind the counter, with attitude says water. So, my mother-in-law asks if they have sprite and the girl just said "water". My mother-in-law said," Y'all don't have anything else?" She shook her head saying no. We have water. My husband walks back up and says "ma, they have coke, I'm looking right at it." The girls response was, "well if you knew that then why'd you even ask?" My husband told her his mother was asking and she wasn't telling her. I understand there was a long line (as these girls who were working this station kept saying in front of all of the customers) but they literally just opened the stand and already complaining for everyone to see. As Military, it's hard to believe that there are such unprofessional and disrespectful people out there when literally all they have to do is go to work, put on a smile for a couple hours and sell prepackaged beverages. Other than this, the park was beautiful and there were plenty of photo opportunities. We got our caricature drawn around 7 pm and the lovely artist did an amazing job, I can't wait to hang it up in our house,Thank you for that experience. She was so kind and very professional. We made the most out of our trip so it still turned out amazing but it only takes one bad experience to ruin a good time and that's unfortunate. I hope y'all find this helpful so you aren't disappointed. Have a great trip!

original_text:
This review took a little longer to get done than I'd have liked, but I had so much fun here I wanted to make sure I finished detailing all of the individual rides first.

The first time you visit a theme park you've never been to before is always something special. New rides, new attractions, all sorts of surprises around every corner. Busch Gardens gave me this feeling and then some. I've been to Universal Studios a few times in the past couple years, and while that's always been fun it's a different type of experience. Universal has a focus on 3D/4D motion rides, which again not complaining, but they only have two real roller coasters at the moment. Well, full disclosure, this Yelper right here is roller coaster fanatic, and as soon as I walked in the gates at Busch Gardens I looked at the park map and got a childlike giddiness I haven't had in a while. This place was my playground for the day and I couldn't have been happier.

Busch Gardens Tampa is themed around African places and environments. The Cobra's Curse is in Egypt, while the Cheetah Hunt is in The Edge of Africa and Kumba runs through Congo, with the Serengeti Express running around the whole park. The park does an extremely good job giving off the appropriate vibe for the area they're imitating. Sure there are snack carts and restroom signs, but it's more immersive than just about any other park I've visited. (They're sister park in Williamsburg, VA does the same thing with a European countries idea instead of African theme here.) What I'm trying to say is this place is beautiful. It's an enormous theme park with world class roller coasters and more impressive animal enclosures than your typical zoo. There's almost too much to mention about this wonderful place but I'll try and go for the highlights.

ROLLER COASTERS! Seven incredible coasters with another two being built before 2020. I've visited theme parks all up and down the east coast and some of these stand out among the best. The Cobra's Curse with it's independently rotating carts and crazy elevator lift is easily the most unique coaster I've ever been on, the giant wing and true vertical drop of Sheikra was exhilarating and Cheetah Hunt's crazy launch system, smooth ride and amazing views make it extremely well rounded and a real contender for favorite coaster of all time.

ANIMALS! I know the topic of zoo's can be polarizing for some, and I'm no wildlife expert, but in my humble opinion they handle the idea of animals in captivity better than most places. Alligators, Tigers, Elephants, Flamingos, Kangaroos, Gorillas, Orangutans, Giraffes. Every enclosure is massive and more than allows for the animals to move about and enjoy themselves. (The Serengeti Plain "exhibit" is practically a third of the whole park.) They're are at least three different areas of the park where you can get very intimate with tropical birds (while supervised by park staff). I can't say I've been anywhere else that I've been inches away from a brilliant Peacock and later the same day get bit by a Parakeet and watch a Kookaburra nesting. It really is something special.

It's also worth mentioning that my visit was just before Christmas and when the sun goes down you get to see just how elaborate their lights and decorations are for the season. Traditional Christmas bulbs adorned the train station while tunnels of elaborate LEDs illuminated the paths between park sections and projectors display giant snowflakes over the gates. They go above and beyond to feel festive.

If there is anything that could be described as a "con" against the massive list of "pros" to this place it would be that they do fall victim to the curse of theme park food. Our small samplings during the day were fairly tasty, but the prices are a bit steep, the exception being the all day unlimited refill souvenir cup (which was a life saver).

Busch Gardens may not be the most famous Florida theme park, but just because the pair of giants down in Orlando get all the attention doesn't mean this place can't stand on its own. It depends on what you're looking for in a theme park experience, but for me the cheaper ticket prices, far superior roller coaster selection and the plethora of animals make this place an instant win. I fully intend to make this place a recurring destination.

I always expect theme parks to have long lines and be quite expensive, but this was by far the worst experience I've had yet.

I made my reservation and tried to buy my tickets online ahead of time, but the 1/2-off coupons would only work at the self-service ticketing. When I arrived and tried this, the machines wouldn't take them, and I had to wait 45+ minutes in the ticket line. When I finally arrived at the window to get my tickets and the Unlimited Quick Que pass, the employee informed me that the QQ price had been increased to $130 each! On their website and in all their advertising, QQ's are listed as only $20. Nowhere online does it say that their prices are subject to change or that they WILL jack up the prices on busy days.

When we finally got to our first ride, I was told that I couldn't bring/wear my cross-body bag in line and would need to purchase a locker! Bags are allowed in the park, but they removed the bins outside rides for loose items, so you are forced to pay even more to store your belongings once inside the park!

After 2+ hours getting tickets, finding out I couldn't have a bag, going back to my car to ditch it, then getting back into the park, we finally reached the roller coasters only to find out that even with the Quick Que ticket, we had to wait 45+ for the rides. Never in my life have we had to wait this long with Quick Q/Fast Pass tickets and because of the lines, we couldn't even take full advantage of the Unlimited Quick Que!

I want to start off this review with the fact that I have been to over 20 amusement/theme parks around the country (Cedar Point, Magic Mountain, Great Adventure, Universal Parks, Disney Parks, Hersheypark, Kings Island, etc.) and Busch Gardens and the Sea World Company has provided the worst experience I have ever had at an amusement park. I would strongly advise considering other attractions such as the Disney or Universal Parks, or the Tampa Zoo if you are in the area.

Our visit included 2 standard tickets for $90 each, 2 Quick Queue standard passes for $100 each, and $80 spent on food and water for one meal. This is all before any sales tax, and incudes Busch Garden's lovely 5% commission tax they place on every product or service they have.

Our day was pretty much a complete rainout (7/17/23) and featured rides operational for just under 2 hours and an early park close. I want to clarify that this alone does not depict the 1 star score, as weather is out of the parks control and I don't fault parks for operating safely. My real issues come in terms of the operation of the park itself.

To start, during our brief time of operations the quick queue system is completely broken. There was no one to check our quick queue in front of the two rides we were able to get on (Cheetah Hunt & Montu). We experienced several times people entering the line who did not purchase fast passes and made our $200+ purchase of Quick Queue null and void when we had to still wait over an hour on a weekday due to horribly slow operations and a lack of care from employees. We noticed employees visibly high on drugs and not actively checking restraints on rides (Montu). Their operation team brought a girl to tears as she was concerned her safety harness was not locked in place. Their operations were on average between 5-10 minutes per train without using all trains they had available. Lines easily stacked up to the 2 hour point during our brief operation period.

The food quality was some of the lowest and the most expensive I have ever purchased. Yes, Disney and Universal both have cheaper food options by far. We ordered 2 plates of chicken tenders and fries with one large drink and our purchase came out to $50. When using the standard of a Tyson Chicken Tender, this was far below the mark when factoring how undercooked and little food you get for that steep price. Yes, let me repeat the fact they charge a 5% charge on any food, services, or souvenirs you purchase. We had to wait an hour at a kiosk by Kumba (roller coaster) during inclement weather to purchase a drink and fan because their computer wasn't working and security did not want to walk to that part of the park and help.

Then we get to the part of the day where they decide to close the park 2 hours early just as the radar is showing a clear few hours. We felt very disappointed in spending over $400 for 2 park tickets to ride almost nothing, 2 quick queues that were pointless as they don't properly enforce them, and dealing with safety concern after safety concern. The guest service line was hours long, so I decided to call the hotline to get assistance. I explained the situation that I live 800 miles away and wouldn't be able to visit the Busch Gardens Parks even if I wanted to. I was honestly terrified with how the park was being operated and didn't want to step into another one of their parks and receive a refund.

How does SeaWorld Entertainment try and make a horrible experience up to a returning customer? They offer 2 tickets with 1 year expiration on them. No inclusion of our over $200 spent on fast passes, and no concern about the ability to be able to return to a SeaWorld location within a year. Not everyone has the luxury of being able to go on vacations each year and I don't think SeaWorld understands that and their target audience. After further review, I have come upon forums and forums of people with similar situations and a lack of customer service from the parks. Even for people who bought tickets during COVID, SeaWorld and Busch Gardens used the claim of "unforeseen circumstances" to still not refund customers who never even entered the gates.

I'm very disappointed in what Busch Gardens calls their customer service and their lack of care to try and keep customers returning back to their theme parks. They can keep on adding roller coasters year by year all they want, but the declining crowds and negative experiences people have is what is going to destroy this company if someone doesn't step up their customer service and care for the people that keep this place running.

Please second guess if you are considering visiting one of SeaWorld's many parks (Busch Gardens Tampa/Williamsburg, Sea World Orlando/San Diego/San Antonio, Aquatica, etc.) as they do not have your best interest in mind and only care about thinning your pockets as much as they can. You will get much better value and care at any of the other theme parks in Florida they compete with.

Well I have been going to this place since 1977. I have seen this place change over the years. I have never seen this place so very frustrating to go to. I truly believe when you open your place of business it should be completely opened. You get there the parking lot opens about 9:30am. You pay $28.00 U.S. or $14 if a pass holder. The ticket for 1 day at the gate is $109. So you go to stand in line to get in. At 10:00 am you go in the gates. Can you ride the rides, go to sesame street, or go see animals, no you cannot. You have to wait till 10:30am for some of the earlier attractions to open, even the concession stands aren't open. If you want to ride some of the rides, you have to wait till noon. Some places don't even open till a line forms and the attendant has to finish with another ride. This is Florida it's very hot and humid. If your handicapped and have a parking permit you can get kinda close to the gates sometimes. They are painting the parking lot which could be done at night. Be prepared to go a football field distance before you get to the only gates to enter. Your being robbed of so much when the WHOLE park isn't opened all the way.
If they treat paying customers this way, how are the animals and employees treated?
Don't give a "standard" reply about contacting at an email or a premade answer, like you have done to so many before. Just fix the problem.

We did watch the ice skating show and it was AMAZING!! Overall a great day!!

original_text:
I really enjoyed this park! We went for our first time as a group. I'm happy we bought annual Florida passes because we plan to come back again and also to use it at other parks. My experience coming to this park was as a complete novice. Parking was $25 and filled up quickly! They don't seem to have enough handicapped and/or preferred parking. Come early if you fit into either of these two categories. I like that they have a tram system to take you from the parking lot to the park, however, I had a large stroller and there was no space to put it on the tram without taking up space for other riders. So we walked about 15 minutes to the entrance. At the entrance, as a first timer with annual passes, we were made to wait in line at booth B. I estimate that we waited in line for at least 1.5 hours!!! And we got there around noon. The park opens at 10am. I suggest to go early if you're trying to redeem your printed online passes and free kiddy passes!
After we got our passes we were able to quickly go through security! A plus that there is a metal detector! The guards also look through bags! Good!
Once in the park, it was a joy! The only thing I didn't like was that to exit the park once you're ready to go, the options of where to walk is extremely limited! There's a good supply of bathrooms and rides!
I had a good latter half of my day there! I recommend.

original_text:
Today was my first time going to Busch Gardens. We had reservations to the special event of LUNCH with Elmo and Friends. I was told on the phone it would be inside but be warned it is in an outdoor pavilion. The employee I spoke to was wrong about the set up of the facility. In addition it is self seating so therefore the tables were confusing. For example we were table 11 but there was another family that was also considered "table 11". Therefore it was a bit chaotic when different groups were meeting the characters. Personally I think each family should be assigned a number instead.

There was an outdoor cheer competition happening directly next to our pavilion. Their music was off the charts loud to the point you couldn't hear the person sitting across from you. Therefore the Sesame Street staff did the "logical" thing and tried to blast their music louder than the cheerleaders music. With 2 completely different songs playing at full blast it was absolutely horrible.

The food: The quality of the food was lacking despite my picture not showing the lack of flavor. It honestly tasted comparable to an elementary school's cafeteria. Someone in my group had a roll that was still clearly frozen. There was mashed potatoes, macaroni n cheese, rolls, veggies, chicken strips, pulled pork, salad, cupcakes, and fruit. I tried almost everywhere and was very disappointed.

Characters: First off there is no show during this lunch. I was misinformed over the phone about the entertainment involved during lunch. The characters dance for about 30 seconds. Don't get me wrong it is cute. However I wouldn't pay money just for a few spins. The characters were supposed to walk to one table at a time. However the staff wasn't facilitating enough as people kept approaching the characters, ruining the flow of meet n greets. Our table for completely skipped over by a character and I eventually had to go approach him since they were leaving soon.

Photo: The photographer lady wasn't very friendly at all. I wasn't thrilled with the quality of the photos taken. However 1 of them turned out alright out of about 5.

Favorite character: The count was the best character there for sure. He was fair on the amount of time he spent with each family and I felt he actually invested time with my experience with him. I would've liked to have been able to meet the Cookie Monster or grouch as well. However there was no guaranteed characters except for Elmo.

The cheerleading music interrupted the entire meal and it was honestly miserable. The characters were cute but not $20 a person kind of cute.

I went to guest services and they tried to work out some accommodations due to our poor experience. However I was not satisfied with how things were being handled. I ended up calling Busch garden customer service who attempted to tell me there was nothing left they could do. However I eventually was able to speak with Busch Garden corporate who took my complaints serious. We were able to work out something that I considered to be fair and respectful. The corporate employee I spoke to was kind, empathetic, and made sure things were made right.

Whoever scheduled a cheer competition next to Elmo's special event lunch clearly didn't think through the logistics of sound. The food however does need help, especially for how expensive it was.

I was very disappointed on my experience at Busch Gardens. However clearly corporate does care about customer satisfaction and is willing to take appropriate actions.

It should not have taken 1 guest service trip to speak to a supervisor, being put on hold several times with customer service for a good 30+ minutes, before FINALLY getting someone who could help me out.

So for now Sea World would be my go to place out of the two pairs. Busch Gardens has some growing to do with customer service and with their food.

original_text:
I should have read the reviews before going but I had been to busch gardens Tampa a few years ago and it was amazing. I guess they're trying to make back their revenue losses from covid...

$100 a ticket, paid $45 for preferred parking online only to arrive and be told that parking was full and i'd have to go get a refund. Why would they keep selling preferred parking when they knew it was full? Plus once in the park they didn't even give me a refund, but a $20 park credit which I ended up spending on ONE (1) nasty hotdog and some bottles of water. If they actually cared about customers they'd have water bottle filling stations. The only other food I purchased was 4 chicken tenders and cold fries costing $15.

Here's the kicker: The entire day, never stepped foot on a ride. Some rides had over two hour waits and all others were shut down. On top of that, now you have to PAY to leave your belongings in a locker in order to get on any ride, either $8 for the whole day or $2 a ride I think. It's a small thing but it really made me feel that the park was trying to squeeze out every penny they could. Even the sky ride was closed. I wouldn't be upset if I knew any of this beforehand. Also, bird exhibit where the birds come and land on you: gone.

If you're worried about covid I would HIGHLY suggest not coming here until after the pandemic. They don't take even a quarter of the precautions Disney and Universal do. People wear masks if they feel like it and aren't told to put them on EVER! Seriously saw employees talk to guests without a mask or their mask on their chin and not say anything.

There is absolutely no social distancing in lines AT ALL, I was so uncomfortable because every line I got in was packed with people, EVERY TIME I was in line the person behind me left not even a foot of space between us and ignored floor markings, which was again ignored and not enforced by employees.

The park seemed to be full of the type of people who think masks are a political thing- there was even an altercation while i was in line between an anti masker family and a black family that made myself and the people around me EXTREMELY uncomfortable.

As for the rides:
Lines were pretty long, averaging at about an hour of each of the coasters minus kumba which is closed for refurbishment.

I'd recommend purchasing quick queue tickets if/when you go, but make sure to do so online, as it took an hour and a half for me to get through the line for the ticket window.

Overall wouldn't recommend visiting at this time. Give it a few months to a year, or just go to the parks in Orlando.

This is the most Florida place of my whole visit. It is an amusement park / zoo / ice skating show?

It tries to be everything and ends up being nothing. Compared to most amusement parks in California it comes up far short in every regard.

The attractions are worse.
The food is worse.
The entertainment is more limited.
The shops are more limited.
The staff is less friendly.

I guess if you want to get drunk for cheap and yell at a giraffe from a roller coaster this is the place for you.

I did like the ice skating show, it was quality. Unlike everything else. It did not belong here.

I would have given this 4 stars and rounded up if not for the fact that the kangaroo exhibit is next to the road between the road and a loud rollercoaster. The road frequently had Harley motorcycles making noise scaring the kangaroos into the corner of the exhibit. That was very frustrating to see and is not acceptable. I am not a huge animal rights person, I eat meat, I don't donate all my money to animal rights causes. Still, this was totally unacceptable.

I would always come back here if in the area.

Park was fun, glad I got to ride Sheikra, however the food was awful and I had an unpleasant experience with guest services. Also, the animals looked extremely sad compared to the animals at the Lowry Park Zoo where I'm sure there is a tremendous difference in conservation efforts between the two.

I purchased a "two-park pass" during the "memorial day weekend sale" 50% off. Of course under the fine print the exact weekend the sale was advertising was blacked out. I was let in to Busch Gardens in the morning however was turned away at Adventure Island Water Park. I was told by Gabby at Adventure Island that I could get my two park pass refunded however when I returned to Busch Gardens for the refund Marilyn who seemed to be the manager was rude and not very accommodating to my situation. The situation could have been handled better on the ground level and could have been avoided entirely without the expected shady business practices of Seaworld.

All in all, If you are dying to ride some rollercoasters it could be fun but unless eating an overpriced dogmeat hot dog and looking at depressed komodo dragons integrated with your rollercoaster experience is your thing go to the beach instead. Not returning.
